eyebrow: An experiment in building software with AI agents
headline: Software you can watch grow.
disclaimer: An honest heads-up: this site was written by AI agents. storytree is a one-person project right now — I'm a solo dev and a dad to a 3-year-old, short on spare hours and no great writer, so I've leaned on the very tools I'm building to put it into words and min-max the time I've got. The ideas are mine; the prose is the machine's.
lede: Building software with AI feels like black magic, you just say what you want and the code writes itself. The hard part is understanding what was built and validating it. Storytree turns your project into a living map so you can visualise it while your agents build it. You can still look at the code if you want to, its just how do you know what you want to look at if you dont have a map.

map.eyebrow: A map for you and your AI agents
map.body1: What you're looking at is a software project being built by AI agents — drawn as a living map instead of millions of lines of unverified code. Each tree is one story: a single slice of the product, a piece of behaviour the agents are building.
map.body2: Every line the agents write turns into something you can see here. A bigger island is a more complex part of the system, with more built and tested behind it; a green tree means that part is tested and healthy, while red means a check is failing — where your attention goes next. It's a bird's-eye view of the whole system, and you can drop into any tree to unpack it with your agents. They read the map too — it's how they find their way around the system instead of getting lost in the code.
map.note: Small on purpose. Invite-only for now — but the door to ask is wide open.
